ISO/TC 184/SC 4 Industrial data
ResolutionsMeetingsAbout
ResolutionsMeetingsAboutExternal LinksISO CommitteeLinkedInGitHub

About This Archive

A digital record of 1,456 resolutions from ISO/TC 184/SC 4, spanning 1989 to today.

The Edoxen Format

Resolutions in this archive are stored in plain-text YAML files using the Edoxen format. This structured representation ensures resolutions remain human-readable while being entirely machine-parsable.

metadata:
  title: Resolutions of the plenary meeting of ISO/TC 184/SC 4, Stavanger, Norway
  date: '2024-10-18'
  source: ISO/TC 184/SC 4 Secretariat
  city: NOSVG
  country_code: NO

decisions:
- identifier:
  - prefix: ISO/TC 184/SC 4
    number: '1142'
  kind: resolution
  status: decided
  dates:
  - date: '2024-10-25'
    type: adoption
  localizations:
  - language_code: eng
    title: Set 8-week ballot duration for new work item proposals
    actions:
    - type: requests
      message: |
        SC 4 requests its Committee Manager to apply the
        8-week ballot duration to the following NP ballots...
    considerations:
    - type: noting
      message: |
        SC 4 notes that shorter ballot durations...
    approvals:
    - degree: unanimous
      type: affirmative

Each file contains two main sections: metadata, containing information about the meeting, and a decisions array, detailing the individual decisions made. A decision carries its identifier (StructuredIdentifier with prefix + number), a kind (resolution, order, ruling), and per-language localizations containing the title, subject, considerations, actions, and approvals.

Learn more about Edoxen →

Action Types

Every resolution is composed of typed actions that categorize what the committee decided to do (e.g., requesting an action, approving a document, or thanking a host). This semantic typing allows for advanced filtering and analysis of the committee's historical activities.

requeststhanksappointsapprovesresolvesdirectsasksencouragesacceptsinstructsnominatesdecidesagreesadoptsestablisheswelcomescreatesrecommendsendorsesnotesrecognizesconfirmsappreciationallocatessupportsdisbandsacknowledgesassignsappreciates

URN Identifiers

Resources in this archive are assigned Uniform Resource Names (URNs) to provide persistent, location-independent identifiers.

  • Resolution URNs:urn:iso:tc:184:sc:4:resolution:{id}
    Example: urn:iso:tc:184:sc:4:resolution:1142
  • Meeting URNs:urn:iso:tc:184:sc:4:meeting:{source_file}
    Example: urn:iso:tc:184:sc:4:meeting:plenary-2024-10-stavanger-norway

Note that per RFC 5141, "documents at or below the Technical Committee level" are not covered by the standard urn:iso:std: namespace. Section 2.6 delegates URN management for TC resources to the Technical Committees themselves.

Resolution Lifecycle

Each resolution in the Edoxen model follows a structured lifecycle, captured through three interconnected sections:

1

Considerations

The context and background that led to the resolution. Each consideration has a type (e.g., noting, recalling, recognising) and a message explaining what the committee observed or referenced.

2

Actions

The decisions themselves — what the committee resolved to do. Each action carries a semantic type (e.g., requests, approves, appoints) that categorizes the nature of the decision, along with the detailed message.

3

Approvals

How the resolution was formally adopted, including the degree of consensus (e.g., unanimous, consensus) and any relevant notes about the approval process.

A single resolution may contain multiple considerations, actions, and approvals — together forming a complete record of the committee's decision-making process.

About ISO/TC 184/SC 4

Industrial data

Standardization of the content, meaning, structure, representation and quality management of information for engineered products throughout their lifecycle.

ANSI (Secretariat)
Chair
Kenneth Swope
